metadata.lyricist: Martin Rinkart, c.1636. Translated Catherine Winkworth, 1856
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Martin Rinkart, c.1636. Translated Catherine Winkworth, 1856.  Music: 'Nun Danket' Johann Crüger, 1647.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.

1.raw_text: Now thank we all our God, with heart and hands and voices, Who wondrous things has done, in Whom this world rejoices; Who from our mothers' arms has blessed us on our way With countless gifts of love, and still is ours today.

2.raw_text: O may this bounteous God through all our life be near us, With ever joyful hearts and blessed peace to cheer us; And keep us in His grace, and guide us when perplexed; And free us from all ills, in this world and the next!

3.raw_text: All praise and thanks to God the Father now be given; The Son and Him Who reigns with Them in highest Heaven; The one eternal God, Whom earth and Heav'n adore; For thus it was, is now, and shall be evermore.
